review_goal: Evaluate whether a developer who wants to build agents can understand MEGA Code's value quickly enough to start onboarding with confidence through login or GitHub-based onboarding.
core_journey: Land on the site, understand what MEGA Code does, decide whether it seems useful, and start onboarding through login or GitHub.
business_goal: Increase the rate of qualified developer visitors who start onboarding after understanding the product's practical value.

persona.name: Agent-Building Developer
persona.segment: A developer evaluating new AI agent tools who wants to improve how they build, review, and iterate on agents without wasting time on low-value tooling.
persona.job_to_be_done: Figure out quickly whether MEGA Code will materially help me build better agents and, if yes, start onboarding with low risk and low effort.
persona.context: Usually arrives from a recommendation, social post, or search result while actively exploring agent tooling. Will judge the product quickly and leave if the value is vague or sounds like hype.
goals: Understand what the product actually does
Know whether it will help with real agent-building work
Start onboarding only if the value feels credible
pain_points: AI developer tools often sound impressive but do not improve real output
It is hard to tell quickly whether a new tool will be worth setup time
If value proof is weak before onboarding, trust drops fast
access_needs: Clear plain-English explanation of product value
Low ambiguity in onboarding entry points
persona.success_definition: I understand what this does, why it is different, and why I should sign in now instead of coming back later.
evidence_sources: founder-provided observation about uncertainty around usefulness
known concern that product effect is not obvious before onboarding
developer persona definition focused on agent-building outcomes

evidence: Observed concern: users may not understand whether MEGA Code is actually useful to them before they are asked to onboard.
Observed concern: users may not be able to tell what concrete benefit they will get from logging in or using GitHub onboarding.
Observed concern: users evaluating agent tools are likely to be skeptical and may leave quickly if the site sounds promising but lacks immediate proof.
known_constraints: Limit the review to the landing-page-to-onboarding decision journey.
Prioritize issues affecting trust, clarity, and onboarding start rate.
Do not turn findings into UI pixel-level prescriptions.
notes: Keep persona voice consistent but do not roleplay theatrically.
Every recommendation must explain expected user impact and business impact.
